claim_boundary.allowed: sealed live solution packet for CFTR F508del membrane multidomain folding/rescue mechanism, pending review
claim_boundary.not_allowed: universal protein-folding solved claim, atomic CFTR solution claim, or one-local-mutation-only claim

falsification_criteria: F508del is fully explained by one local residue deletion with no NBD1 stability effect
F508del correction is complete with NBD1 stabilization alone in all relevant maturation/function assays
interdomain/interface evidence does not affect correction or maturation readouts
trafficking/proteostasis readouts do not change despite folding/maturation rescue
generic channel annotation alone predicts the F508del rescue logic
coordinate evidence before sealing is required for the mechanism packet
independent holdouts contradict more than two explicit perturbation predictions

low_resolution_structure_or_ensemble_pre.architecture: full-length multidomain membrane ABC channel with MSD1/NBD1/R/MSD2/NBD2 assembly
low_resolution_structure_or_ensemble_pre.atomic_claim: blocked; no coordinate solution is claimed
low_resolution_structure_or_ensemble_pre.defect_model: F508del causes a coupled NBD1 stability, domain-interface assembly, trafficking/proteostasis, and channel-function defect

operator_region_rationale.F508del_local_destabilization_operator: F508del removes phenylalanine 508 and is annotated as impairing folding and stability.
operator_region_rationale.NBD1_stability_operator: F508 lies in NBD1, whose stability/folding competence is central to the defect grammar.
operator_region_rationale.channel_function_context_operator: Channel opening/function readouts are downstream maturation context, not atomic-fold proof.
operator_region_rationale.corrector_or_rescue_context_operator: Corrector logic should require domain/interface/proteostasis rescue rather than one local patch.
operator_region_rationale.interdomain_interface_coupling_operator: The F508del annotation points to domain-interaction surface changes, not merely a local residue defect.
operator_region_rationale.membrane_domain_operator: P13569 topology annotates two membrane-spanning domains and 12 transmembrane segments.
operator_region_rationale.multidomain_assembly_operator: CFTR is a full-length membrane ABC protein whose folding defect emerges during multidomain assembly.
operator_region_rationale.trafficking_quality_control_operator: F508del impairs maturation and trafficking to the cell membrane and is routed toward degradation.

operator_regions.rationale: P13569 topology annotates two membrane-spanning domains and 12 transmembrane segments.
F508 lies in NBD1, whose stability/folding competence is central to the defect grammar.
F508del removes phenylalanine 508 and is annotated as impairing folding and stability.
The F508del annotation points to domain-interaction surface changes, not merely a local residue defect.
operator_regions.span: NBD1 423-646 coupled to MSD/ICL cytoplasmic interface

operator_regions.rationale: F508del impairs maturation and trafficking to the cell membrane and is routed toward degradation.
Corrector logic should require domain/interface/proteostasis rescue rather than one local patch.
operator_regions.span: NBD1 stability, interface coupling, and proteostasis rescue axes

operator_regions.rationale: CFTR is a full-length membrane ABC protein whose folding defect emerges during multidomain assembly.
Channel opening/function readouts are downstream maturation context, not atomic-fold proof.

perturbation_predictions.predicted_effect: weakens NBD1 stability or folding competence

perturbation_predictions.perturbation: track F508del maturation and glycan processing
perturbation_predictions.predicted_effect: shows multidomain maturation/processing failure rather than a local annotation-only defect
partially rescues folding/maturation but is not necessarily sufficient for full correction
provides stronger correction when combined with NBD1 stabilization
maps to domain/interface/proteostasis grammar rather than magic channel activation
must fail to produce a CFTR F508del solution packet
must be blocked because CFTR is a membrane multidomain ABC protein
weakens the solution and collapses strong correction logic
weakens the solution and reduces F508del defect specificity
weakens maturation grammar while not falsifying atomic fold claims

perturbation_predictions.perturbation: measure rescued channel opening at the membrane
perturbation_predictions.predicted_effect: surface function improves only after folding/maturation rescue; function readout is downstream context

perturbation_predictions.perturbation: combine NBD1, interface, and proteostasis correctors

predicted_defect_grammar.prediction: F508 deletion weakens NBD1 folding/stability competence.
F508del perturbs an NBD1 surface needed for MSD/ICL domain coupling.
Misassembled F508del-CFTR fails efficient glycan maturation and ER-to-membrane trafficking.
Quality-control machinery routes misfolded/misassembled protein toward degradation.
Residual or rescued surface protein may still show altered opening/gating readouts.

predicted_rescue_or_corrector_grammar.prediction: NBD1 stabilizers should improve folding competence but may be partial alone.
Interface/domain-coupling correction should be required for stronger rescue.
Rescue must be assessed through maturation, trafficking, and surface function.
Combinations targeting distinct folding defects should outperform one-axis correction.

proposed_experimental_tests: compare WT and F508del NBD1 stability/folding using non-coordinate biochemical stability assays
measure full-length F508del glycan maturation and ER-to-surface trafficking with and without NBD1 stabilizers
test interface/domain-coupling suppressors or correctors for synergy with NBD1 stabilization
compare corrector classes for NBD1-only, interface, and proteostasis/maturation rescue signatures
measure rescued surface channel opening separately from maturation rescue
ablate proteostasis/quality-control components and quantify maturation versus degradation changes

sequence_region_scope.focus: F508del in NBD1 / ABC transporter 1 domain
sequence_region_scope.label: full-length CFTR P13569 with F508del focus in NBD1

target: human CFTR F508del membrane multidomain folding defect
